channel.title: Internet Systems Consortium (ISC) — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Internet Systems Consortium (ISC) (isc).

item.title: CVE-2026-5950 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Unbounded resend loop in BIND 9 resolver
item.description: An unbounded resend loop vulnerability exists in the BIND 9 resolver state machine during bad-server handling, enabling a remote unauthenticated attacker to cause severe resource exhaustion by sending queries that trigger specific retry conditions. T…

item.title: CVE-2026-5947 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — SIG(0) validation during query flood may lead to undefined behavior
item.description: Undefined behavior may result due to a race condition leading to a use-after-free violation. If BIND receives an incoming DNS message signed with SIG(0), it begins work to validate that signature. If, during that validation, the "recursive-clients" l…

item.title: CVE-2026-5946 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Invalid handling of CLASS != IN
item.description: Multiple flaws have been identified in `named` related to the handling of DNS messages whose CLASS is not Internet (`IN`) — for example, `CHAOS` or `HESIOD`, or DNS messages that specify meta-classes (`ANY` or `NONE`) in the question section. Special…

item.title: CVE-2026-3593 · 7.4 HIGH 🌩️ — Heap use-after-free vulnerability in BIND 9 DNS-over-HTTPS implementation
item.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists within the DNS-over-HTTPS implementation. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.20.0 through 9.20.22, 9.21.0 through 9.21.21, and 9.20.9-S1 through 9.20.22-S1. BIND 9 versions 9.18.0 through 9.18.48 and 9.18.11-S1…

item.title: CVE-2026-3592 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Amplification vulnerabilities via self-pointed glue records
item.description: BIND resolvers are vulnerable to an amplified resource consumption/exhaustion attack. If a victim resolver makes a query to a specially crafted zone, the resolver will consume disproportionate resources. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.11.0 thro…

item.title: CVE-2026-3039 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — BIND 9 server memory exhaustion during GSS-API TKEY negotiation
item.description: BIND servers that are configured to use TKEY-based authentication via GSS-API tokens are vulnerable to excessive memory consumption when receiving and processing maliciously-constructed packets. Typically these servers will be found in Active Directo…

item.title: CVE-2026-3591 · 5.4 MEDIUM — A stack use-after-return flaw in SIG(0) handling code may enable ACL bypass
item.description: A use-after-return vulnerability exists in the `named` server when handling DNS queries signed with SIG(0). Using a specially-crafted DNS request, an attacker may be able to cause an ACL to improperly (mis)match an IP address. In a default-allow ACL…

item.title: CVE-2026-3119 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Authenticated query containing a TKEY record may cause named to terminate unexpectedly
item.description: Under certain conditions, `named` may crash when processing a correctly signed query containing a TKEY record. The affected code can only be reached if an incoming request has a valid transaction signature (TSIG) from a key declared in the `named` co…

item.title: CVE-2026-3104 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Memory leak in code preparing DNSSEC proofs of non-existence
item.description: A specially crafted domain can be used to cause a memory leak in a BIND resolver simply by querying this domain. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.20.0 through 9.20.20, 9.21.0 through 9.21.19, and 9.20.9-S1 through 9.20.20-S1. BIND 9 versions 9.18…

item.title: CVE-2026-1519 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Excessive NSEC3 iterations cause high CPU load during insecure delegation validation
item.description: If a BIND resolver is performing DNSSEC validation and encounters a maliciously crafted zone, the resolver may consume excessive CPU. Authoritative-only servers are generally unaffected, although there are circumstances where authoritative servers ma…

item.title: CVE-2026-3608 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Stack overflow in Kea daemons
item.description: Sending a maliciously crafted message to the kea-ctrl-agent, kea-dhcp-ddns, kea-dhcp4, or kea-dhcp6 daemons over any configured API socket or HA listener can cause the receiving daemon to exit with a stack overflow error. This issue affects Kea versi…

item.title: CVE-2025-13878 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Malformed BRID/HHIT records can cause named to terminate unexpectedly
item.description: Malformed BRID/HHIT records can cause `named` to terminate unexpectedly. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.18.40 through 9.18.43, 9.20.13 through 9.20.17, 9.21.12 through 9.21.16, 9.18.40-S1 through 9.18.43-S1, and 9.20.13-S1 through 9.20.17-S1.

item.title: CVE-2025-11232 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Invalid characters cause assert
item.description: To trigger the issue, three configuration parameters must have specific settings: "hostname-char-set" must be left at the default setting, which is "[^A-Za-z0-9.-]"; "hostname-char-replacement" must be empty (the default); and "ddns-qualifying-suffix…

item.title: CVE-2025-40780 · 8.6 HIGH 🌩️ — Cache poisoning due to weak PRNG
item.description: In specific circumstances, due to a weakness in the Pseudo Random Number Generator (PRNG) that is used, it is possible for an attacker to predict the source port and query ID that BIND will use. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.16.0 through 9.16.…

item.title: CVE-2025-40778 · 8.6 HIGH 🌩️ — Cache poisoning attacks with unsolicited RRs
item.description: Under certain circumstances, BIND is too lenient when accepting records from answers, allowing an attacker to inject forged data into the cache. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.11.0 through 9.16.50, 9.18.0 through 9.18.39, 9.20.0 through 9.20.13…

item.title: CVE-2025-8677 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Resource exhaustion via malformed DNSKEY handling
item.description: Querying for records within a specially crafted zone containing certain malformed DNSKEY records can lead to CPU exhaustion. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.18.0 through 9.18.39, 9.20.0 through 9.20.13, 9.21.0 through 9.21.12, 9.18.11-S1 through…

item.title: CVE-2025-8696 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — DoS attack against the Stork UI from an unauthenticated user
item.description: If an unauthenticated user sends a large amount of data to the Stork UI, it may cause memory and disk use problems for the system running the Stork server. This issue affects Stork versions 1.0.0 through 2.3.0.

item.title: CVE-2025-40779 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Kea crash upon interaction between specific client options and subnet selection
item.description: If a DHCPv4 client sends a request with some specific options, and Kea fails to find an appropriate subnet for the client, the `kea-dhcp4` process will abort with an assertion failure. This happens only if the client request is unicast directly to Ke…

item.title: CVE-2025-40777 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — A possible assertion failure when 'stale-answer-client-timeout' is set to '0'
item.description: If a `named` caching resolver is configured with `serve-stale-enable` `yes`, and with `stale-answer-client-timeout` set to `0` (the only allowable value other than `disabled`), and if the resolver, in the process of resolving a query, encounters a CN…

item.title: CVE-2025-40776 · 8.6 HIGH 🌩️ — Birthday Attack against Resolvers supporting ECS
item.description: A `named` caching resolver that is configured to send ECS (EDNS Client Subnet) options may be vulnerable to a cache-poisoning attack. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.11.3-S1 through 9.16.50-S1, 9.18.11-S1 through 9.18.37-S1, and 9.20.9-S1 throug…

item.title: CVE-2025-32803 · 4 MEDIUM — Insecure file permissions can result in confidential information leakage
item.description: In some cases, Kea log files or lease files may be world-readable. This issue affects Kea versions 2.4.0 through 2.4.1, 2.6.0 through 2.6.2, and 2.7.0 through 2.7.8.

item.title: CVE-2025-32802 · 6.1 MEDIUM — Insecure handling of file paths allows multiple local attacks
item.description: Kea configuration and API directives can be used to overwrite arbitrary files, subject to permissions granted to Kea. Many common configurations run Kea as root, leave the API entry points unsecured by default, and/or place the control sockets in ins…

item.title: CVE-2025-32801 · 7.8 HIGH — Loading a malicious hook library can lead to local privilege escalation
item.description: Kea configuration and API directives can be used to load a malicious hook library. Many common configurations run Kea as root, leave the API entry points unsecured by default, and/or place the control sockets in insecure paths. This issue affects Kea…

item.title: CVE-2025-40775 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — DNS message with invalid TSIG causes an assertion failure
item.description: When an incoming DNS protocol message includes a Transaction Signature (TSIG), BIND always checks it. If the TSIG contains an invalid value in the algorithm field, BIND immediately aborts with an assertion failure. This issue affects BIND 9 versions…

item.title: CVE-2024-12705 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — DNS-over-HTTPS implementation suffers from multiple issues under heavy query load
item.description: Clients using DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH) can exhaust a DNS resolver's CPU and/or memory by flooding it with crafted valid or invalid HTTP/2 traffic. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.18.0 through 9.18.32, 9.20.0 through 9.20.4, 9.21.0 through 9.21.3, an…

item.title: CVE-2024-11187 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Many records in the additional section cause CPU exhaustion
item.description: It is possible to construct a zone such that some queries to it will generate responses containing numerous records in the Additional section. An attacker sending many such queries can cause either the authoritative server itself or an independent re…

item.title: CVE-2024-4076 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Assertion failure when serving both stale cache data and authoritative zone content
item.description: Client queries that trigger serving stale data and that also require lookups in local authoritative zone data may result in an assertion failure. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.16.13 through 9.16.50, 9.18.0 through 9.18.27, 9.19.0 through 9.19.…

item.title: CVE-2024-1975 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — SIG(0) can be used to exhaust CPU resources
item.description: If a server hosts a zone containing a "KEY" Resource Record, or a resolver DNSSEC-validates a "KEY" Resource Record from a DNSSEC-signed domain in cache, a client can exhaust resolver CPU resources by sending a stream of SIG(0) signed requests. This…

item.title: CVE-2024-1737 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — BIND's database will be slow if a very large number of RRs exist at the same name
item.description: Resolver caches and authoritative zone databases that hold significant numbers of RRs for the same hostname (of any RTYPE) can suffer from degraded performance as content is being added or updated, and also when handling client queries for this name.…

item.title: CVE-2024-0760 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — A flood of DNS messages over TCP may make the server unstable
item.description: A malicious client can send many DNS messages over TCP, potentially causing the server to become unstable while the attack is in progress. The server may recover after the attack ceases. Use of ACLs will not mitigate the attack. This issue affects BI…

item.title: CVE-2024-28872 · 8.9 HIGH 🌩️ — Incorrect TLS certificate validation can lead to escalated privileges
item.description: The TLS certificate validation code is flawed. An attacker can obtain a TLS certificate from the Stork server and use it to connect to the Stork agent. Once this connection is established with the valid certificate, the attacker can send malicious co…

item.title: CVE-2023-6516 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Specific recursive query patterns may lead to an out-of-memory condition
item.description: To keep its cache database efficient, `named` running as a recursive resolver occasionally attempts to clean up the database. It uses several methods, including some that are asynchronous: a small chunk of memory pointing to the cache element that ca…

item.title: CVE-2023-5680 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Cleaning an ECS-enabled cache may cause excessive CPU load
item.description: If a resolver cache has a very large number of ECS records stored for the same name, the process of cleaning the cache database node for this name can significantly impair query performance. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.11.3-S1 through 9.11.3…

item.title: CVE-2023-5679 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Enabling both DNS64 and serve-stale may cause an assertion failure during recursive resolution
item.description: A bad interaction between DNS64 and serve-stale may cause `named` to crash with an assertion failure during recursive resolution, when both of these features are enabled. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.16.12 through 9.16.45, 9.18.0 through 9.18…

item.title: CVE-2023-5517 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Querying RFC 1918 reverse zones may cause an assertion failure when "nxdomain-redirect" is enabled
item.description: A flaw in query-handling code can cause `named` to exit prematurely with an assertion failure when: - `nxdomain-redirect <domain>;` is configured, and - the resolver receives a PTR query for an RFC 1918 address that would normally result in an author…

item.title: CVE-2023-4408 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Parsing large DNS messages may cause excessive CPU load
item.description: The DNS message parsing code in `named` includes a section whose computational complexity is overly high. It does not cause problems for typical DNS traffic, but crafted queries and responses may cause excessive CPU load on the affected `named` insta…

item.title: CVE-2023-4236 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — named may terminate unexpectedly under high DNS-over-TLS query load
item.description: A flaw in the networking code handling DNS-over-TLS queries may cause `named` to terminate unexpectedly due to an assertion failure. This happens when internal data structures are incorrectly reused under significant DNS-over-TLS query load. This iss…

item.title: CVE-2023-3341 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — A stack exhaustion flaw in control channel code may cause named to terminate unexpectedly
item.description: The code that processes control channel messages sent to `named` calls certain functions recursively during packet parsing. Recursion depth is only limited by the maximum accepted packet size; depending on the environment, this may cause the packet-p…

item.title: CVE-2023-2911 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Exceeding the recursive-clients quota may cause named to terminate unexpectedly when stale-answer-client-timeout is set to 0
item.description: If the `recursive-clients` quota is reached on a BIND 9 resolver configured with both `stale-answer-enable yes;` and `stale-answer-client-timeout 0;`, a sequence of serve-stale-related lookups could cause `named` to loop and terminate unexpectedly du…

item.title: CVE-2023-2829 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Malformed NSEC records can cause named to terminate unexpectedly when synth-from-dnssec is enabled
item.description: A `named` instance configured to run as a DNSSEC-validating recursive resolver with the Aggressive Use of DNSSEC-Validated Cache (RFC 8198) option (`synth-from-dnssec`) enabled can be remotely terminated using a zone with a malformed NSEC record. Thi…

item.title: CVE-2023-2828 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — named's configured cache size limit can be significantly exceeded
item.description: Every `named` instance configured to run as a recursive resolver maintains a cache database holding the responses to the queries it has recently sent to authoritative servers. The size limit for that cache database can be configured using the `max-ca…

item.title: CVE-2022-3924 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — named configured to answer from stale cache may terminate unexpectedly at recursive-clients soft quota
item.description: This issue can affect BIND 9 resolvers with `stale-answer-enable yes;` that also make use of the option `stale-answer-client-timeout`, configured with a value greater than zero. If the resolver receives many queries that require recursion, there will…

item.title: CVE-2022-3736 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — named configured to answer from stale cache may terminate unexpectedly while processing RRSIG queries
item.description: BIND 9 resolver can crash when stale cache and stale answers are enabled, option `stale-answer-client-timeout` is set to a positive integer, and the resolver receives an RRSIG query. This issue affects BIND 9 versions 9.16.12 through 9.16.36, 9.18.0…

item.title: CVE-2022-3488 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — named may terminate unexpectedly when processing ECS options in repeated responses to iterative queries
item.description: Processing of repeated responses to the same query, where both responses contain ECS pseudo-options, but where the first is broken in some way, can cause BIND to exit with an assertion failure. 'Broken' in this context is anything that would cause th…

item.title: CVE-2022-3094 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — An UPDATE message flood may cause named to exhaust all available memory
item.description: Sending a flood of dynamic DNS updates may cause `named` to allocate large amounts of memory. This, in turn, may cause `named` to exit due to a lack of free memory. We are not aware of any cases where this has been exploited. Memory is allocated prio…

item.title: CVE-2022-2929 · 6.5 MEDIUM — DHCP memory leak
item.description: In ISC DHCP 1.0 -> 4.4.3, ISC DHCP 4.1-ESV-R1 -> 4.1-ESV-R16-P1 a system with access to a DHCP server, sending DHCP packets crafted to include fqdn labels longer than 63 bytes, could eventually cause the server to run out of memory.

item.title: CVE-2022-2928 · 6.5 MEDIUM — An option refcount overflow exists in dhcpd
item.description: In ISC DHCP 4.4.0 -> 4.4.3, ISC DHCP 4.1-ESV-R1 -> 4.1-ESV-R16-P1, when the function option_code_hash_lookup() is called from add_option(), it increases the option's refcount field. However, there is not a corresponding call to option_dereference() t…

item.title: CVE-2022-3080 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — BIND 9 resolvers configured to answer from stale cache with zero stale-answer-client-timeout may terminate unexpectedly
item.description: By sending specific queries to the resolver, an attacker can cause named to crash.

item.title: CVE-2022-38178 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Memory leaks in EdDSA DNSSEC verification code
item.description: By spoofing the target resolver with responses that have a malformed EdDSA signature, an attacker can trigger a small memory leak. It is possible to gradually erode available memory to the point where named crashes for lack of resources.

item.title: CVE-2022-38177 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Memory leak in ECDSA DNSSEC verification code
item.description: By spoofing the target resolver with responses that have a malformed ECDSA signature, an attacker can trigger a small memory leak. It is possible to gradually erode available memory to the point where named crashes for lack of resources.
